ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_code" TEXT /* Code for the country (e.g., AU) */,
  "english_name" TEXT /* English name of the country (e.g., Australia) */,
  "spanish_name" TEXT /* Spanish name of the country (e.g., Australia) */,
  "french_name" TEXT /* French name of the country (e.g., Australie) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "currency_code" TEXT /* Currency code used in the country (e.g., AUD) */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Primary timezone of the country (e.g., UTC+10) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "states_provinces" (
  "state_province_code" TEXT /* Code for the state or province (e.g., NSW) */,
  "state_province_name" TEXT /* Name of the state or province (e.g., New South Wales) */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Code of the country the state or province belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state or province */,
  "capital_city_id" INTEGER /* ID of the capital city of the state or province */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_province_code", "country_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_states_provinces_capital_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("capital_city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city (e.g., Alexandria) */,
  "state_province_code" TEXT /* Code of the state or province the city belongs to */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Code of the country the city belongs to */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_country_code FOREIGN KEY ("country_code") REFERENCES countries ("country_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_state_province_code FOREIGN KEY ("state_province_code") REFERENCES states_provinces ("state_province_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_territories" (
  "sales_territory_key" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales territory */,
  "sales_territory_name" TEXT /* Name of the sales territory */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Code of the country the sales territory belongs to */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager responsible for the sales territory */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Annual revenue of the sales territory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sales_territory_key"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_territories_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_territories_country_code FOREIGN KEY ("country_code") REFERENCES countries ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "ip_addresses" (
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* ID of the city the IP address belongs to */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Code of the country the IP address belongs to */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude associated with the IP address */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude associated with the IP address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_address"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_addresses_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_addresses_country_code FOREIGN KEY ("country_code") REFERENCES countries ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, query) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
